dashboard.subtitle1: Tools, tips and support for people with ADHD.
dashboard.subtitle2: Everyone deserves a chance to focus in a way that works for them.
dashboard.iosInstallHint: On iPhone: tap Share then "Add to Home Screen" to install the app.
dashboard.installNotAvailableHint: Install is not available yet in this browser. Make sure you're on HTTPS and the app is fully loaded, then try again.

dashboard.footer: Built with love for the ADHD community.
dashboard.feature1_desc: Simple time-management techniques tailored for people with ADHD. Pomodoro, task breakdown and more.
dashboard.feature2_desc: A clear, simple task system that helps you track progress without feeling overwhelmed.
dashboard.feature3_desc: Join a community of people who get it. Share, learn and grow with others facing the same challenges.

focus.title: Focus mode
focus.subtitle: ADHD-friendly Pomodoro timer
focus.noStepsYet: No steps defined for this task yet.

focus.tip1: Put your phone face down and away
focus.tip3: Drink a glass of water before starting
focus.tip4: Write the task on paper in front of you
focus.tip5: Tell someone you're in focus mode

dopamine.title: Dopamine boost
dopamine.subtitle: Science-based tips for motivation and energy
dopamine.scienceDesc1: Dopamine is a neurotransmitter responsible for motivation, reward and attention. In the ADHD brain, the dopamine system works differently – so it's harder to start and sustain tasks.
dopamine.scienceDesc2: ADHD is linked to lower dopamine levels in specific brain circuits. This creates a constant search for strong, immediate stimulation.
dopamine.scienceDesc3: Movement, music, small wins, sunlight, social connection, creativity and certain foods – all naturally and healthily boost dopamine.
dopamine.habitTip: Don't try to change everything at once! Pick one small habit and do it for a week. One small success creates a wave of change.
dopamine.researchDesc: Studies show that people with ADHD benefit from regular exercise, enough sleep, and good nutrition – sometimes as much as from medication. Small lifestyle changes can change everything.
dopamine.tipSunDesc: 10-15 minutes of direct daylight can raise dopamine and help set your biological clock.
dopamine.tipMoveDesc: 5-10 minutes of walking or light jumps can boost dopamine and motivation for hours.
dopamine.tipMusicDesc: Listening to enjoyable music releases dopamine and improves focus.
dopamine.tipWinDesc: Finish one small task - your brain gets a dopamine reward and wants more.
dopamine.tipWaterDesc: Staying hydrated supports brain function and healthy dopamine levels.
dopamine.tipSocialDesc: A short conversation or a hug can raise dopamine and help you calm down.

dailyTasksNotification.bodyEmpty: No open tasks for today. Well done.

calm.title: Calm & balance
calm.subtitle: Self-regulation exercises for ADHD
calm.breathIntro: Controlled breathing activates the parasympathetic nervous system and calms within minutes
calm.emotionDesc: People with ADHD experience emotions more intensely. It's normal and manageable. The trick is to notice the emotion early and use calming tools before it overwhelms.
calm.tip1Desc: Find 5 things you see, 4 you hear, 3 you feel, 2 you smell, 1 you taste. Anchors you in the present.
calm.tip2Desc: Splash cold water on your face or put an ice cube on your wrist. Activates the vagus nerve and calms.
calm.tip3Desc: Put a hand on your heart, take a deep breath and say: 'I'm okay, I'm safe'. Helps regulate emotions.
calm.tip4Desc: Listen to a calming song you love. Slow music (60 BPM) syncs the heart and calms the nervous system.
calm.tip5Desc: 5 minutes outside, preferably in nature or near trees. Nature lowers cortisol in proven ways.

aiHelp.errorEmpty: To get a tailored solution, briefly describe your problem.

aiHelp.encouragement: You don't have to finish everything now, just get the momentum going.

dailyMotivation.title: Daily sentence
dailyMotivation.sentence1: A small step today is real progress, even if it's not perfect.
dailyMotivation.sentence2: You don't need perfect motivation, just one small start.
dailyMotivation.sentence3: Every task you finish builds confidence for tomorrow.
dailyMotivation.sentence4: Focus is a muscle. Every minute of practice strengthens it.
dailyMotivation.sentence5: Today it's enough to be 1% better than yesterday.
dailyMotivation.sentence6: Deep breath, a short start, and watch how it moves forward.
dailyMotivation.sentence7: Even a deliberate pause is part of healthy attention management.
dailyMotivation.sentence8: Small wins add up to big change over time.
dailyMotivation.sentence9: You're not behind, you're on your own path at your own pace.
dailyMotivation.sentence10: Pick one task now. That's all it takes to move forward.
